# Scorched-Earth Erdős Sweep — Turn 98

This is turn 98. Turns 1 through 97 are consolidated in `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` (attached). Re-read it before answering.
Also re-read `mega_prompt.md` and `problem_statement.md`.

CRITICAL — use ONLY the files attached to THIS message. Do NOT open, import, retrieve, or cite any file from your File Library / previously uploaded files, even one named similarly (e.g. another `turns_history…md`). Other unrelated problems use similarly named history files in the same account, and importing one will silently switch you onto the wrong problem. If the attached `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` appears missing, do NOT continue from partial context. Reply only with `MISSING_REQUIRED_ATTACHMENT` and name the missing file(s); never substitute a library file.

If this turn drifts into hammering one specific case or sub-instance, step back and ask whether the case is revealing a structural pattern that would close many cases at once. Prefer arguments whose conclusion is a uniform statement.

End this response with ESTABLISHED / GAP / NEXT + tag.
Continue with Turn 98.

## Turn 98 — two-input closure of the fixed-frame proof

The verifier submission remains:

```text
sigma,tau,x0,x1

sigma2 = sigma^40491355905
u0 = (x0*tau)^40491355905
u1 = (x1*tau)^40491355905
d0 = u0*x0^-1
z0 = x0^sigma2
c0 = d0^-1*z0^-1*d0*z0

x0^2*x1^4*u1^-1*(x1^sigma)*c0
```

The tame relation \(\tau^\sigma=\tau^2\) is implicit. In the mathematical proof, the exponent denotes the genuine idempotent \(\omega _2\in\widehat{\mathbb Z}\); \(40491355905\) is only its required residue modulo \(85667662080\). This is exactly the submission convention in the attached specification. 

Turn 97 reduced the proof to two standard inputs, with finite-Heisenberg duality and all distribution arguments now internal.  Here they are in the exact forms needed.

### Input T — ordinary Thompson criticality

For every finite \(2\)-group \(K\), there is a characteristic subgroup \(C\le K\) such that

\[
C/Z(C)\ \text{is elementary abelian},\qquad [C,K]\le Z(C),
\]

and

\[
\ker\!\bigl(\operatorname{Aut}(K)\to\operatorname{Aut}(C)\bigr)
\]

is a \(2\)-group.

Set

\[
D=\Omega _2(C)=\langle c\in C:c^4=1\rangle .
\]

The internal argument from Turn 87 then gives

\[
D^4=1,\qquad [D,K]\le Z(D),\qquad
\ker\!\bigl(\operatorname{Aut}(K)\to\operatorname{Aut}(D)\bigr)
\text{ is a \(2\)-group}.
\tag{98.1}
\]

Consequently, in the minimal active block of the fixed-tame-frame induction, the odd tame action must act nontrivially on \(D\). Minimality forces \(D=K\), and hence

\[
K^4=1,\qquad [K,K]\le Z(K).
\]

Therefore

\[
R=\Phi(K)=K^2[K,K]
\]

is central elementary abelian in \(K\), while

\[
M=K/R
\]

is elementary abelian. This is the only use of Thompson criticality. 

### Input K — dyadic Kummer/Hilbert package

Let \(V\) be a nontrivial simple tame \(\mathbb F _2\)-module for \(G_{\mathbb Q _2}\), and suppose \(V\) is self-dual with invariant nonsingular quadratic form \(q\). Let

\[
Q_{\mathrm{loc}}:H^1(\mathbb Q _2,V)\longrightarrow
H^2(\mathbb Q _2,\mathbb F _2)\simeq\mathbb F _2
\]

be the connecting quadratic form of the associated central extension.

For a finite tame splitting field \(K/\mathbb Q _2\), with tame image \(H\), use:

\[
H^1(\mathbb Q _2,V)
 \simeq
\operatorname{Hom}_H\!\left(V^\vee,K^\times/K^{\times2}\right);
\tag{98.2}
\]

the odd-depth unit filtration on \(K^\times/K^{\times2}\);

the perfect pairings of the nontrivial-inertia pieces at depths \(j\) and \(2e-j\);

\[
U_i^\perp=U_{2e-i+1};
\tag{98.3}
\]

and

\[
a\smile a=a\smile(-1).
\tag{98.4}
\]

For ramified \(V\), exactness through the filtration gives the deep-unit space

\[
X_+=\operatorname{Hom}_H(V^\vee,U_{e+1})
\]

with

\[
\dim X_+=\frac12\dim H^1(\mathbb Q _2,V).
\]

Equations (98.3)–(98.4) make \(X_+\) totally singular for \(Q_{\mathrm{loc}}\). Hence the ramified form is hyperbolic.

For unramified \(V\), the nontrivial isotypic Kummer space is one-dimensional over the endomorphism field \(D\), and the invariant form is the norm–trace form

\[
x\longmapsto
\operatorname{Tr}_{D_0/\mathbb F _2}(a\,x x^\ast).
\]

Its zero count is the minus-type count. Thus, writing \(d=\dim_{\mathbb F _2}V\),

\[
N_0(Q_{\mathrm{loc}})
=
\begin{cases}
2^{d-1}-2^{d/2-1},&V\ \text{unramified},\\[2mm]
2^{d-1}+2^{d/2-1},&V\ \text{ramified}.
\end{cases}
\tag{98.5}
\]

The candidate gives the same two values. If \(S,T\) are the tame actions and \(U=S^{\omega _2}\), its determinant obstruction is

\[
Q_A(c)=
\begin{cases}
q(c),&T=1,\\
q(c)+B(c,U^{-1}c),&V^T=0.
\end{cases}
\tag{98.6}
\]

The internal Wall-doubling identity

\[
\operatorname{Arf}\!\left(q+B(-,U^{-1}-)\right)
=
\operatorname{Arf}(q)+\operatorname{rank}(1+U)\pmod2
\tag{98.7}
\]

makes (98.6) minus type in the unramified case and hyperbolic in the ramified case. Therefore

\[
N_i(Q_A)=N_i(Q_{\mathrm{loc}}),\qquad i=0,1.
\tag{98.8}
\]

This is precisely the local input isolated at the end of Turn 97. 

## Standalone fixed-frame theorem

Let \(G\) be finite, \(L\triangleleft G\) a normal \(2\)-subgroup, and

\[
H=G/L.
\]

Fix a complete tame frame

\[
\alpha:T_{\mathrm{tame}}\twoheadrightarrow H,
\qquad
T_{\mathrm{tame}}
=\langle\sigma,\tau\mid\tau^\sigma=\tau^2\rangle,
\]

together with any elementary scalar decoration.

For either source

\[
\Gamma\in\{\Gamma_A,G_{\mathbb Q _2}\},
\]

let \(E_\Gamma(J)\) denote the decorated, fixed-frame maps whose exact image is a specified subgroup \(J\le G\) projecting onto \(H\).

Then

\[
|E_{\Gamma_A}(J)|=|E_{G_{\mathbb Q _2}}(J)|
\tag{98.9}
\]

for every such \(J\).

### Proof

Induct strongly on the order of the marked wild kernel.

If every wild chief factor is scalar, the target is the canonical pullback of the fixed tame quotient and a finite \(2\)-group. Its complete exact-image poset is consequently determined by the common marked maximal pro-\(2\) quotient

\[
\left\langle
\sigma,x_0,x_1
\ \middle|\
x_0^2x_1^3x_1^\sigma=1
\right\rangle_{\mathrm{pro}\text{-}2}.
\tag{98.10}
\]

This proves all terminal exact-image strata simultaneously.

Otherwise, choose the first nontrivial tame chief factor above the scalar radical and the associated minimal active subgroup \(K\). Input T gives

\[
R=\Phi(K)\ \text{central elementary},\qquad M=K/R\ \text{elementary}.
\]

Moreover, with \(T_0=(K\cap S)/R\),

\[
T_0=\operatorname{rad}(M),\qquad
M/T_0\simeq V,\qquad
(M^\vee)^{G/K}=0.
\tag{98.11}
\]

For the \(M\)-lift, the common-word finite-Heisenberg complex gives perfect duality for every elementary module, including nonsemisimple \(M\):

\[
H_\rho^i(M)\times H_\rho^{2-i}(M^\vee)\to\mathbb F _2,
\]

and hence

\[
\operatorname{Ob}_\rho(M)^\vee\simeq(M^\vee)^{G/K}=0,
\qquad
\dim Z_\rho^1(M)=2\dim M.
\tag{98.12}
\]

Thus every lower map has the same total \(M\)-lift multiplicity on both sides. Exact proper-image strata have strictly smaller marked wild kernels and are equal by induction. The naturality and extension-dé­vissage needed here are now internal consequences of evaluating one common finite word complex. 

It remains to lift through \(R\). Put

\[
D=(R^\vee)^{G/R}.
\]

Finite-Heisenberg duality identifies \(D\) with the complete character group of the \(R\)-valued obstruction. Fourier inversion reduces the obstruction-zero count to scalar pushouts indexed by \(\lambda\in D\).

For \(\lambda\ne0\), the simple-head lemma gives two possibilities:

- a nonzero filtration-one edge, which is a nonzero affine functional and therefore has an exact half-zero fibre;
- a determinant component on the simple head \(V\).

In the determinant case, nonsplit layers are killed by transgression; split mixed edge terms are removed by the marking-preserving shear. The unrestricted determinant fibre is then governed by (98.8).

Generation and the inflated base bit are both controlled by a single transform. For any marked central cover

\[
p:\widetilde Y\twoheadrightarrow Y
\]

with kernel \(C_2\), and any exact-image stratum \(J\le Y\),

\[
8\,u_\Gamma(p,J)
=
\sum_{\substack{\widetilde J\le\widetilde Y\\p(\widetilde J)=J}}
|E_\Gamma(\widetilde J)|.
\tag{98.13}
\]

The right side consists entirely of ordinary exact-image strata in strictly smaller marked targets. Hence it agrees by induction.

Applying (98.13) first to proper obstruction-weighted image strata and then to the inflated determinant constant yields the unrestricted formula

\[
Z_{\Gamma,\lambda}
=
\mu\Bigl(
N_1(Q)\,|X_\Gamma|
+
\bigl(N_0(Q)-N_1(Q)\bigr)n_{\Gamma,0}
\Bigr),
\tag{98.14}
\]

where every factor agrees between the two sources:

- \(\mu\) by common-word deformation duality;
- \(N_0,N_1\) by (98.8);
- \(|X_\Gamma|\) by lower exact-image induction;
- \(n_{\Gamma,0}\) by the central-cover transform.

Thus every Fourier coefficient agrees, so the complete \(R\)-obstruction has the same zero fibre. Once it vanishes, both sources have the same \(R\)-lift multiplicity. Every such lift is surjective because \(R=\Phi(K)\).

This completes the strong exact-image induction and proves (98.9). The central-cover identity is the uniform mechanism that handles both weighted proper images and the sheared inflated constant. 

## Consequence

Taking \(J=G\), summing over all tame frames, removing decorations, and setting

\[
L=O_2(G)
\]

gives

\[
|\operatorname{Sur}(\Gamma_A,G)|
=
|\operatorname{Sur}(G_{\mathbb Q _2},G)|
\]

for every finite group \(G\). Epimorphisms from the cofiltered marked object \(\Gamma_A\) are exactly the generating tuples used by the official verifier. Equality of all finite surjection counts then gives the profinite isomorphism

\[
\Gamma_A\simeq G_{\mathbb Q _2}.
\]

The private 5402-group verifier has not been executed here. Also, under the attachment-only restriction, I cannot supply the missing bibliographic location for Input T; the exact theorem needed is now stated above, so this is a citation gap rather than an unidentified proof branch.

**ESTABLISHED**: Stated the two remaining standard inputs in the exact forms used and inserted them into a standalone, noncircular fixed-frame exact-image theorem. The Turn-97 common-word dévissage and Turn-96 central-cover transform now appear explicitly at their only points of use. Conditional on ordinary Thompson criticality and the stated standard dyadic Kummer/Hilbert package, the unchanged \(A_2\) block has the same surjection count as \(G_{\mathbb Q _2}\) for every finite target.

**GAP**: The private 5402-group verifier remains unrun, and the allowed attachments contain no exact bibliographic citation for the ordinary Thompson critical-subgroup theorem.

**NEXT**: Run the unchanged residue block through the official verifier and attach a precise published citation for Input T.

[BREAKTHROUGH]
